issues.title: Migrate's settings.json rewrite repoints commands for hooks it never renamed, silently killing custom hooks
issues.description: Reported from a consuming Instance (evolvco/create-evolve-internal, 2026-06-12): after the .cjs hook-layout migration, their custom push-guard.js/merge-guard.js were silently dead — settings.json pointed at .cjs files that do not exist. Mechanism confirmed in src/bin/cli.js migrateHookLayout: the file-rename step is hash-guarded (untracked and locally-modified hooks are skipped and reported) but the HOOK_COMMAND_REWRITE pass then repoints every .claude/hooks/*.js substring in settings.json unconditionally ('Done whatever the hook outcome'), including commands whose target files were just skipped. An enforcement hook dying silently is the worst failure mode. Fix end-to-end: reproduce in a temp Instance carrying a custom hook, a locally-modified stamped hook, and a recorded-hash marker; then scope the settings rewrite to exactly the files migrate renamed in this or a prior run, and report — never rewrite — commands that point at skipped or untracked .js hooks. Also confirm or refute the stale-twins hypothesis: a marker without recorded file hashes classes every stamped hook as untracked, so migrate renames nothing while update stamps fresh .cjs copies, leaving a .js twin of every stamped hook (the consumer's second symptom).
acceptance_criteria: Repro exists: a temp Instance with a custom .js hook, a locally-modified stamped .js hook, and a hash-recording marker demonstrates the defect before the fix and its absence after
Migrate rewrites a settings.json command only when its target hook file was renamed by migrate (this run or a completed prior run); idempotency on re-run holds
A settings command pointing at a skipped or untracked .js hook is reported by name in migrate's output, and the file keeps working (command still names the existing .js)
The stale-twins hypothesis (hash-less marker classes all hooks untracked → update stamps .cjs beside .js) is confirmed or refuted in the repro, and the finding recorded in this issue's fix field
package-qa PASS including a new migrate-contract check covering the custom-hook and modified-hook cases
issues.root_cause: migrateHookLayout's settings rewrite scope (every .claude/hooks/*.js substring) is broader than its file-rename scope (only hooks byte-identical to their recorded stamped form)
issues.fix: Scoped migrateHookLayout's settings.json rewrite to hooks migrate renamed (this run, or a completed prior run leaving .cjs with no .js twin): HOOK_COMMAND_REWRITE became a recognizer with capture groups (migrate-policy.js); cli.js decides per match via hookCommandMigrated/rewriteHookCommands; commands naming skipped or untracked .js hooks are reported by name and left firing against the existing .js. Stale-twins hypothesis CONFIRMED: a marker without a files map yields recordedHash undefined for every hook, classing all stamped hooks untracked, so migrate renames nothing and a subsequent update stamps fresh .cjs copies beside the orphaned .js — verified live in the repro. package-qa migrate-contract check extended (clean/modified/custom hook trio, idempotency, interrupted-run repoint, healthy no-op).
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-12 from the create-evolve-internal agent's report (via Brooks). Consumer self-fixed in their PR #41: renamed guards to .cjs, repointed tests, removed twins, 23/23 hook tests green. Framework regression EXEC-075/076.

issues.title: Doctor names dead settings commands and .js/.cjs hook twins
issues.description: The create-evolve-internal breakage stayed invisible until a guard failed to fire: nothing in the framework flags a settings.json hook command pointing at a file that does not exist, or a stamped hook present as both .js and .cjs. Extend doctor's per-feature hook health: report each settings command whose target file is missing (BROKEN, naming the command and the missing path) and each hook present under both extensions (naming the twin and which file settings actually invokes). Covers custom commands the Instance added, not just stamped features — a dead enforcement hook is exactly what doctor exists to surface.
acceptance_criteria: doctor reports a settings.json hook command whose target file is missing, naming the command and path — including commands for non-stamped (custom) hooks
doctor reports a hook present as both <name>.js and <name>.cjs, naming which one settings invokes
A healthy migrated Instance (all .cjs, commands aligned) reports no new findings
package-qa PASS with doctor checks extended to cover both conditions
issues.fix: doctor gained two Instance-wide report lines after hook layout: 'hook commands:' resolves every settings.json hook command against disk and reports BROKEN naming the command and the missing path, custom non-stamped commands included; 'hook files:' reports each hook present as both <name>.js and <name>.cjs, naming which extension settings invokes (.cjs/.js/both/neither). readHookCommands extracted as the shared settings-command flattener (wiringProblems behavior unchanged); HOOK_COMMAND_SCRIPT_PATTERN added to health-policy.js. A healthy migrated Instance reports neither finding, exit 0. package-qa doctor checks extended to both conditions.
issues.notes: Split from the EXEC-086 report at breakdown review (Brooks, 2026-06-12): detection is its own slice; doctor stays the health authority.

issues.title: Stamp a CLI quick-reference README into the invariant layout
issues.description: A stamped Instance carries no in-repo pointer to the Scaffolder verbs, so users and agents must run npx to-execution --help or read the package source to discover init/update/migrate/doctor/view-status/validate. Observed on longrunning-agents: npm install alone stamps nothing and nothing in the repo said so. Add .excn/README.md to the Template (src/template/.excn/README.md) — a light quick-reference: one line per verb (init, update, migrate, doctor, view-status, validate), leading with the gotcha that npm install only downloads the package and init does the stamping. Stamped by init as an invariant file, delivered to old Instances by update, root Instance mirrored Src-First. Precise Minimalism: instruction only, target ≤30 lines.
acceptance_criteria: src/template/.excn/README.md exists, ≤30 lines, covering all six CLI verbs (init, update, migrate, doctor, view-status, validate) one line each, and states that npm install alone stamps nothing — init does
init on a fresh target writes .excn/README.md; update on a previously-stamped Instance adds it
Verb descriptions match the CLI's --help text (no drift in what each verb claims to do)
Root dogfood Instance mirrors the stamped copy (Src-First)
package-qa PASS (manifest grows by the new template file; pack parity holds)
issues.root_cause: The invariant layout documents process (PROCESS.md) and domain (CONTEXT.md) but never the Scaffolder CLI that maintains it
issues.fix: Added src/template/.excn/README.md (13 lines): one line per CLI verb matching --help text, leading with the npm-install-stamps-nothing gotcha. Classifies invariant (stamped by init, restored by update), ships via the existing template/ manifest entry, root copy mirrored byte-identically (Src-First). Executed by team-lead per TEAM_DIRECTIVE src/template ownership.
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-12 (Brooks), motivated by the longrunning-agents viewer confusion: npm install @latest produced no visible change and no in-repo doc explained the init/update path.

issues.title: Setup Grill content moves into the Setup Skill bundle; execution-grill-with-docs serves the Work Grill only
issues.description: The Setup Skill (src/SKILL.md) delegates its Setup Grill context step to the stamped execution-grill-with-docs, whose rules are Work-Grill-specific — it feeds a PRD and forbids writing PHILOSOPHY.md — and both are wrong during setup, where Handoff follows and the philosophy layer legitimately writes PHILOSOPHY.md. Move the setup-interview content (context layer, philosophy sweep, team, one-off adherence agents) into the Setup Skill's own npm bundle: inline in src/SKILL.md if it stays within Precise Minimalism, else a bundled setup-grill reference beside principles.md (npm files manifest grows). The context layer references the stamped CONTEXT-FORMAT.md by path — stamping (step 2) precedes the grill (step 3). The Setup Skill stops invoking execution-grill-with-docs; that skill stays purely the Work Grill's first step, unchanged. Explicit no-s from the 2026-06-12 Work Grill: no sentinel hook, no config.json rename, no migrate step, and no new stamped skill — the Setup Grill content rides in the to-execution package/plugin bundle, never stamped into Instances. Primary audience (Brooks, 2026-06-12): the fresh adopter arriving via npm — npm install to-execution, then telling the agent to read and follow node_modules/to-execution/SKILL.md. Write the skill for that reader: no assumption of a skill registry, an installed personal skill, or prior framework context. Skill-registry and plugin invocations read the same file and lose nothing.
acceptance_criteria: src/SKILL.md's Setup Grill phase carries its own interview instructions and no longer invokes or references execution-grill-with-docs
SKILL.md works standalone for an agent reading it from node_modules/to-execution/ with no skill registry and no prior context — it plus its bundled references is the complete setup procedure
The setup context layer references the stamped CONTEXT-FORMAT.md by explicit path
If a bundled reference file is added, the npm files manifest ships it (pack parity holds)
src/template/.claude/skills/execution-grill-with-docs/ is byte-unchanged by this issue
package-qa PASS
issues.root_cause: The Setup Grill reused the Work Grill's first-step skill for its context layer, so one skill carried two conflicting rule sets selected by a caller it cannot see
issues.notes: Resolved in the 2026-06-12 Work Grill (Brooks). Sentinel-hook + config.json variants were considered and descoped by the lead; ADR-0009 was drafted for the config rename and withdrawn with the descope.

issues.title: Doc sweep: the two grills' descriptions stop implying a shared skill
issues.description: After EXEC-083, sweep the persistent docs that describe the Setup Grill as running execution-grill-with-docs: the root Instance's .excn/CONTEXT.md (Setup Skill and Setup Grill / Work Grill glossary entries), .excn/PROCESS.md 'Two grills, kept separate' and their src/template seeds where the wording appears. Each grill's docs name only its own skill(s): the Setup Grill is the to-execution skill end-to-end; the Work Grill is execution-grill-with-docs → execution-to-prd → execution-to-issues. Src-First where a swept file has a template counterpart.
acceptance_criteria: No persistent doc states or implies the Setup Grill runs execution-grill-with-docs
CONTEXT.md glossary entries (Setup Skill, Setup Grill / Work Grill) match the post-EXEC-083 reality
Template seeds and root copies agree (Src-First)
Chartered-change gates pass (alignment on persistent-doc edits)
issues.notes: Split from EXEC-083 at breakdown review (Brooks, 2026-06-12).
